label: Couchbase
description: Connect to a Couchbase cluster or Capella cloud database using a connection string, username, password, and bucket name.

form.title: Connect Couchbase
form.subtitle: Provide your cluster connection string, credentials, and bucket to start querying your Couchbase data.

methods.label: Connection String
methods.description: Paste your Couchbase connection string (couchbase:// or couchbases://) along with your username, password, and target bucket. Works for both self-managed clusters and Couchbase Capella.
methods.how_to:
## Connect with a Couchbase Connection String

This is the fastest path for both self-managed Couchbase Server and Couchbase Capella (cloud).

### Self-managed Couchbase Server

1. Open the Couchbase Web Console (typically `http://<your-node>:8091`).
2. Your connection string is `couchbase://<hostname-or-ip>` (unencrypted) or `couchbases://<hostname-or-ip>` (TLS, recommended for production).
3. Use the username and password of a Couchbase user that has at minimum **Data Reader** or **Data Writer** roles on the target bucket. Create users under **Security → Users → Add User**.
4. Enter the bucket name you want Anton to query (visible under **Buckets** in the console).

### Couchbase Capella (cloud)

1. Log in to [Capella](https://cloud.couchbase.com) and open your **Cluster**.
2. Go to **Connect** → copy the **Connection String**. It looks like `couchbases://cb.<id>.cloud.couchbase.com`.
3. Under **Database Access**, create or use an existing database credential (username + password) with appropriate bucket permissions.
4. Make sure your IP address is listed under **Allowed IP Addresses** in the cluster's **Connect** tab.
5. Enter the target bucket name from the **Buckets** tab.

### Notes

- Use `couchbases://` (with an **s**) for TLS-encrypted connections — required for Capella and recommended for production clusters.
- The password is stored encrypted locally and never sent to Anton's servers.
- If the connection times out, verify that the Couchbase port (11210 for unencrypted, 11207 for TLS) is open in your firewall/security group.

fields.name: connection_string
fields.label: Connection String
fields.description: Use couchbase:// for unencrypted or couchbases:// for TLS. For Capella, copy this from the Connect tab of your cluster.

fields.name: user
fields.label: Username
fields.description: Couchbase database user with access to the target bucket.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password for the Couchbase database user above.

fields.name: bucket
fields.label: Bucket Name
fields.description: The name of the Couchbase bucket Anton should connect to.

fields.name: scope
fields.label: Scope (optional)
fields.description: The scope within the bucket. Leave as _default unless you use custom scopes.

fields.name: collection
fields.label: Collection (optional)
fields.description: The collection within the scope. Leave as _default unless you use named collections.

fields.name: tls_verify
fields.label: Verify TLS Certificate
fields.description: Disable only if using a self-signed certificate on a self-managed cluster. Always leave enabled for Capella.
